---
title: "Final Project of Divvy Bikes"
author: "Cecilia Zhong"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        % Enable wrapping for Pandoc code blocks
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{makecell}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{commandchars=\\\{\}}
editor: source
execute-dir: project
execute:
  echo: true
---


```{python}
# setup
import os
import geopandas as gpd
from os.path import join
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import altair as alt
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
import numpy as np
import time
import warnings
import seaborn as sns
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
alt.renderers.enable('png')
data_path = 'data/external'

# improve graph resolution
import tempfile
from IPython.display import SVG, display, Image
import vl_convert as vlc

def display_altair_png(chart, scale=2):
    """
    Render an Altair chart to a PNG and display it inline.

    Parameters
    ----------
    chart : altair.Chart
        Altair chart object to render.
    scale : int, optional
        Resolution scaling factor for the PNG (default = 2). Use scale=2 for standard slides. Use scale = 3–4 for dense figures or PDF exports.
    """
    png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(chart.to_dict(), scale=scale)

    # Write to a temporary PNG file and display
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".png") as tmp:
        tmp.write(png_bytes)
        tmp.flush()
        display(Image(filename=tmp.name))
```

```{python}
# =========================
# Paths
# =========================
DATA_PATH = 'data_final/derive_data/divvy_202506_cleaned.parquet'
BOUNDARY_PATH = 'data_final/city/Boundary_City.geojson'
RAIL_PATH = 'data_final/CTA_transportation/CTA_Rail.geojson'  # use your existing rail geojson

# =========================
# 1. Load data
# =========================
print("Loading data...")
df = pd.read_parquet(DATA_PATH)

df['started_at'] = pd.to_datetime(df['started_at'])
df['hour'] = df['started_at'].dt.hour
df['day_of_week'] = df['started_at'].dt.dayofweek
df['is_weekend'] = df['day_of_week'].isin([5,6])

# =========================
# 2. Time block classification
# =========================
def time_block(h):
    if 6 <= h < 10:
        return "Morning commute"
    elif 10 <= h < 16:
        return "Daytime activity"
    elif 16 <= h < 23:
        return "Evening leisure"
    else:
        return None

df['time_block'] = df['hour'].apply(time_block)
df = df.dropna(subset=['time_block'])

# =========================
# 3. Keep unique origin locations (deduplicate coordinates)
# =========================
# Ensure coordinates are numeric
df['start_lat'] = pd.to_numeric(df['start_lat'], errors='coerce')
df['start_lng'] = pd.to_numeric(df['start_lng'], errors='coerce')

unique_points = (
    df[['start_lat','start_lng','time_block','is_weekend']]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
)

print("Unique start locations:", len(unique_points))

# =========================
# 4. Load Chicago boundary + rail lines
# =========================
boundary = gpd.read_file(BOUNDARY_PATH)

# Load rail (geojson / shp)
rail = gpd.read_file(RAIL_PATH)

# Ensure CRS consistency (project rail to boundary CRS)
try:
    rail = rail.to_crs(boundary.crs)
except Exception:
    # If boundary has no CRS or both are already EPSG:4326, continue
    rail.crs = boundary.crs
# =========================
# 5. Plot maps (save individually)
# =========================
print("Plotting maps individually...")

plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')

blocks = ["Morning commute","Daytime activity","Evening leisure"]

for block in blocks:
    for weekend_flag in [False, True]:

        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))

        # Base map
        boundary.plot(
            ax=ax,
            facecolor='#F5F5F5',
            edgecolor='#333333',
            linewidth=1,
            zorder=1
        )

        # Rail overlay
        rail.plot(
            ax=ax,
            color='#2C3E50',
            linewidth=0.9,
            alpha=0.8,
            zorder=2
        )

        # Filter points
        sub = unique_points[
            (unique_points['time_block'] == block) &
            (unique_points['is_weekend'] == weekend_flag)
        ]

        # Plot points
        ax.scatter(
            sub['start_lng'],
            sub['start_lat'],
            s=2.5,
            alpha=0.45,
            color='#E64A19',
            zorder=3
        )

        label = "Weekend" if weekend_flag else "Weekday"

        ax.set_title(f"{block} — {label}",
                     fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

        ax.set_xlim(-87.95, -87.50)
        ax.set_ylim(41.64, 42.03)
        ax.set_aspect('equal')
        ax.axis('off')

        # filename (clean string)
        block_clean = block.replace(" ", "_").lower()
        outname = f"spatial_analysis/{block_clean}_{label.lower()}.png"

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(outname, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
        plt.close()

        print("Saved:", outname)

```

```{python}
trips = pd.read_parquet(DATA_PATH)

display(trips.head())

stations = gpd.read_file('data_final/divvy_bike/Divvy_Bicycle_Stations_20260224.geojson')

stations_clean = stations[[
    'station_name',
    'short_name',
    'latitude',
    'longitude',
    'total_docks',
    'docks_in_service',
    'status',
    'geometry',
]].copy()

stations_clean['total_docks'] = stations_clean['total_docks'].astype(int)

```


```{python}

tract_income = gpd.read_file("data_final/illinois_tract_income/illinois_tract_income.shp")

tract = gpd.read_file("data_final/city/CensusTractsTIGER2010_20260301.geojson")

tract_income = tract_income.rename(columns={
    'ASQPE001': 'median_income',
    'ASQPM001': 'mean_income'
})

tract_income = tract_income.to_crs(epsg=4326)

income_col = tract_income[['GEOID','median_income','mean_income']]

```

```{python}
#| eval: False
#| echo: True


trips_gpd = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    trips,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(trips['start_lng'], trips['start_lat']),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

trips_tract = trips_gpd.sjoin(
    tract,
    predicate='within'
)

trips_tract_count = (
    trips_tract
    .dropna(subset=['geoid10']) 
    .groupby('geoid10')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='n_trips')
)


tract_usage = tract.merge(
    trips_tract_count,
    on='geoid10',
    how='left'
)

tract_usage['n_trips'] = tract_usage['n_trips'].fillna(0)

```


```{python}
#| eval: False
#| echo: True
stations_tract = stations_clean.sjoin(
    tract,
    predicate='within'
)

bicycle_count_tract = (
    stations_tract
    .dropna(subset=['geoid10'])
    .groupby('geoid10')
    .agg(n_bikes=('total_docks','sum'))
    .reset_index()
)

tract_usage = tract_usage.merge(
    bicycle_count_tract,
    on='geoid10',
    how='left'
)

tract_usage['n_bikes'] = tract_usage['n_bikes'].fillna(0)

tract_usage.head()

```

```{python}
#| eval: False
#| echo: True
income_col.head()

tract_usage1 = tract_usage.merge(
    income_col,
    left_on='geoid10',
    right_on='GEOID',
    how='left'
)
tract_usage1.head()

tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['n_bikes'] == 0, 'n_bikes'] = np.nan
tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['median_income'] < 0, 'median_income'] = np.nan
tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['mean_income'] < 0, 'mean_income'] = np.nan

tract_usage1['bike_utilization'] = tract_usage1['n_trips'] / tract_usage1['n_bikes']

q99 = tract_usage1['bike_utilization'].quantile(0.99)

tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['bike_utilization'] > q99, 'bike_utilization'] = q99

q99 = tract_usage1['n_bikes'].quantile(0.99)

tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['n_bikes'] > q99, 'n_bikes'] = q99

q99 = tract_usage1['n_bikes'].quantile(0.99)

tract_usage1.loc[tract_usage1['n_bikes'] > q99, 'n_bikes'] = q99

tract_usage1.describe()
```

```{python}
#| eval: False
#| echo: True
bike_routes = gpd.read_file('data_final/CTA_transportation/Bike_Routes.geojson')

bike_routes = bike_routes.to_crs(tract.crs)

tract_lane = gpd.sjoin(
    tract,
    bike_routes,
    how='left',
    predicate='intersects'
)


tract_lane_count = (
    tract_lane
    .groupby('geoid10')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='n_lanes')
)

tract_lane_count.head()

tract_usage2 = tract_usage1.merge(
    tract_lane_count,
    on='geoid10',
    how='left'
)

tract_usage2['n_lanes'] = tract_usage2['n_lanes'].fillna(0)

#q99 = tract_usage2['n_lanes'].quantile(0.99)

#tract_usage2.loc[tract_usage2['n_lanes'] > q99, 'n_lanes'] = q99

tract_usage2.describe()

tract_usage2_out = tract_usage2.to_crs(epsg=4326)

tract_usage2_out.to_file(
    "data_final/derive_data/tract_usage2.geojson",
    driver="GeoJSON"
)

print("Saved to data_final/derive_data/tract_usage2.geojson")
```

```{python}
tract_usage2 = gpd.read_file('data_final/derive_data/tract_usage2.geojson')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,10))

plot = tract_usage2.plot(
    column='n_trips',
    cmap='viridis',
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='white',
    legend=True,
    legend_kwds={
        "shrink": 0.6,
        "aspect": 30
    },
    ax=ax
)

cbar = plot.get_figure().axes[1]

cbar.set_ylabel("")

cbar.set_title("Total Trips (June 2025)", fontsize=10, pad=10)

ax.set_title("Chicago Divvy Bike Trips by Census Tract", fontsize=14)
ax.axis('off')

plt.savefig("spatial_analysis/n_trips_map.png",
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches='tight')

plt.show()
```

```{python}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,10))

plot = tract_usage2.plot(
    column='n_bikes',
    cmap='viridis',
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='white',
    legend=True,
    legend_kwds={
        "shrink": 0.6,   
        "aspect": 30     
    },
    missing_kwds={
        "color": "lightgrey",
        "label": "No data"
    },
    ax=ax
)

cbar = plot.get_figure().axes[1]

cbar.set_ylabel("")

cbar.set_title("Total Bikes (Dock Capacity)", fontsize=10, pad=10)

ax.set_title("Chicago Divvy Bike Amount by Census Tract", fontsize=14)
ax.axis('off')

plt.savefig("spatial_analysis/n_bikes_map.png",
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches='tight')

plt.show()
```

```{python}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,10))

plot = tract_usage2.plot(
    column='bike_utilization',
    cmap='viridis',
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='white',
    legend=True,
    legend_kwds={
        "shrink": 0.6,
        "aspect": 30
    },
    missing_kwds={
        "color": "lightgrey",
        "label": "No data"
    },
    ax=ax
)

cbar = plot.get_figure().axes[1]

cbar.set_ylabel("")

cbar.set_title("Trips per Bike (Utilization)", fontsize=10, pad=10)

ax.set_title("Chicago Divvy Bike Utilization by Census Tract", fontsize=14)
ax.axis('off')

plt.savefig("spatial_analysis/bike_utilization_map.png",
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches='tight')

plt.show()
```

```{python}
base = alt.Chart(tract_usage2).encode(
    x=alt.X('median_income:Q',
            title='Median Income ($)',
            scale=alt.Scale(zero=False)),
    y=alt.Y('bike_utilization:Q',
            title='Bike Utilization (Trips per Bike)',
            scale=alt.Scale(zero=False))
)

points = base.mark_circle(
    size=40,
    opacity=0.35,
    color="#1f77b4"
)

trend = base.transform_regression(
    'median_income',
    'bike_utilization'
).mark_line(color='red')

chart = (points + trend).properties(
    title='Income vs Bike Utilization (Census Tract)',
    width=600,
    height=400
)

chart.save("spatial_analysis/income_vs_utilization.png")

chart
```

```{python}
base = alt.Chart(tract_usage2).encode(
    x=alt.X('median_income:Q',
            title='Median Income ($)',
            scale=alt.Scale(zero=False)),
    y=alt.Y('n_bikes:Q',
            title='Total Bikes (Dock Capacity)',
            scale=alt.Scale(zero=False))
)

points = base.mark_circle(
    size=40,
    opacity=0.35,
    color="#2ca02c"
)

trend = base.transform_regression(
    'median_income',
    'n_bikes'
).mark_line(color='red')

chart1 = (points + trend).properties(
    title='Income vs Bike Amount (Census Tract)',
    width=600,
    height=400
)

chart1.save("spatial_analysis/income_vs_bikes.png")

chart1
```

```{python}
base = alt.Chart(tract_usage2).encode(
    x=alt.X(
        "median_income:Q",
        title="Median Income ($)",
    ),
    y=alt.Y(
        "n_trips:Q",
        title="Total Trips",
    )
)

points = base.mark_circle(
    size=40,
    opacity=0.35,
    color="#1f77b4"
)

trend = base.transform_regression(
    "median_income",
    "n_trips"
).mark_line(color="red")

chart2 = (points + trend).properties(
    title="Income vs Trips Amount (Census Tract)",
    width=600,
    height=400
)

chart2.save("spatial_analysis/income_vs_trips.png")

chart2
```

```{python}
base = alt.Chart(tract_usage2).encode(
    x=alt.X(
        'n_bikes:Q',
        title='Total Bikes (log scale)',
    ),
    y=alt.Y(
        'bike_utilization:Q',
        title='Trips per Bike',
        scale=alt.Scale(zero=False)
    )
)

points = base.mark_circle(
    size=40,
    opacity=0.35,
    color="#9467bd"
)

trend = base.transform_regression(
    'n_bikes',
    'bike_utilization'
).mark_line(color='red')

chart3 = (points + trend).properties(
    title='Bikes Amount vs Bike Utilization (Census Tract)',
    width=600,
    height=400
)

chart3.save("spatial_analysis/bikes_vs_utilization.png")

chart3
```


```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,10))

plot = tract_usage2.plot(
    column='n_lanes',
    cmap='viridis',
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='white',
    legend=True,
    legend_kwds={
        "shrink": 0.6,
        "aspect": 30
    },
    missing_kwds={
        "color": "lightgrey",
        "label": "No data"
    },
    ax=ax
)

cbar = plot.get_figure().axes[1]

cbar.set_ylabel("")

cbar.set_title("Number of Bike Lanes", fontsize=10, pad=10)

ax.set_title("Chicago Divvy Bike Lanes by Census Tract", fontsize=14)
ax.axis('off')

plt.savefig("spatial_analysis/n_lanes_map.png",
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches='tight')

plt.show()

```

```{python}
chart4 = (
    alt.Chart(tract_usage2)
    .mark_bar()
    .encode(
        x=alt.X('n_lanes:O', title='Bike Lanes Group'),
        y=alt.Y('median(bike_utilization):Q',
                title='Median Bike Utilization'),
        color=alt.Color('lane_group:O', legend=None)
    )
    .properties(
        title='Bike Lanes vs Median Utilization',
        width=600,
        height=400
    )
)

chart4.save("spatial_analysis/lanes_vs_utilization.png")

chart4
```
